items.title: Crafted with GPT image 2 + Gemini Omni/ Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Create a clean, premium storyboard
items.description: Crafted with GPT image 2 + Gemini Omni/ Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Create a clean, premium storyboard infographic for a product ASMR unboxing video. Design specifications: White background with a modern, minimalist layout. Tit
items.prompt: Crafted with GPT image 2 + Gemini Omni/ Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Create a clean, premium storyboard infographic for a product ASMR unboxing video. Design specifications: White background with a modern, minimalist layout. Title at the top: STORYBOARD Product name in bold green (replace with: [PRODUCT NAME]) Subtitle: ASMR UNBOXING Include four information boxes below the title: Duration: 30 Seconds Style: POV Hands, ASMR Audience: 18–35 years old Audio Suggestion: Soft ASMR • Crinkles • Tapping • Pouring Create 12 storyboard panels arranged in a 3-column × 4-row grid. Each panel should contain: A numbered green badge (1–12) Timestamp (0–2.5s, 2.5–5s, etc.) A realistic cinematic image showing the scene Handwritten white doodle text like: TA-DA! POP! WOW! PICK! CRINKLE~ TEAR! POUR~ HOT WATER STIR~ SMOOTH! BEST! Three text sections below every image: VISUAL: ACTION: DIALOGUE: Use realistic product photography, warm wooden table, soft natural lighting, shallow depth of field, POV hands only (no face visible), premium commercial advertising style. Storyboard sequence: 1 (0–2.5s) Visual: Product box on wooden table. Action: Hands enter frame and point to the box. Dialogue: "Have you tried this before?" 2 (2.5–5s) Visual: Opening the box. Action: Lift the top flap slowly. Dialogue: "Let's unbox it together." 3 (5–7.5s) Visual: Multiple individual sachets neatly arranged. Action: Fully reveal the inside. Dialogue: "Wow, look how many!" 4 (7.5–10s) Visual: Pick up one sachet. Action: Remove a single stick pack. Dialogue: "Let's start with one." 5 (10–12.5s) Visual: Close-up of sachet. Action: Hold and gently crinkle it. Dialogue: "That crinkle sound is satisfying." 6 (12.5–15s) Visual: Tear open the top. Action: Slow ASMR tearing. Dialogue: "Just a small tear." 7 (15–17.5s) Visual: Powder visible inside. Action: Show the powder close-up. Dialogue: "Looks delicious." 8 (17.5–20s) Visual: Pour powder into mug. Action: Slow pouring. Dialogue: "Pour everything in." 9 (20–22.5s) Visual: Hot water being poured. Action: Steam visible. Dialogue: "Add hot water." 10 (22.5–25s) Visual: Stir drink. Action: Slow circular stirring. Dialogue: "Mix it well." 11 (25–27.5s) Visual: Finished drink. Action: Show creamy texture from above. Dialogue: "Looks smooth and creamy." 12 (27.5–30s) Visual: Product box beside finished drink with thumbs up. Action: Hand gives thumbs up. Dialogue: "Easy, delicious, and ready!" Footer: Total Duration: 30 Seconds Format: Vertical (9:16) Style: POV Hands, ASMR Unboxing Typography should resemble a professional creative agency storyboard, with clean spacing, green accents, rounded panel borders, and high-end presentation suitable for client pitches.

items.title: Video editing has nowadays become a conversation.. you drop raw footage in a folder, tell Claude
items.description: Video editing has nowadays become a conversation.. you drop raw footage in a folder, tell Claude Code what you want, and get final.mp4 back. its free, open source, 17,000 stars on Github.. it's called video-use. and the
items.prompt: Video editing has nowadays become a conversation.. you drop raw footage in a folder, tell Claude Code what you want, and get final.mp4 back. its free, open source, 17,000 stars on Github.. it's called video-use. and the reason it's different from every "AI video editor" is how it actually works under the hood.. it doesn't guess. it transcribes every word you said first, then edits off the actual transcript. what it does once your footage is in the folder: - cuts every umm, uh, and false start automatically - kills the dead space between takes - color grades each segment.. warm cinematic, neutral punch, or a custom ffmpeg chain - builds the cut with editor sub-agents, runs the animations in parallel - renders a preview, grades its own work, and iterates until it's clean and yeah.. it asks before it cuts. it shows you the plan in plain english and waits for your yes. it's not a black box that spits out a mystery edit.. you stay the director, it does the labor. ▫️ how to start install the skill in claude code (repo below)  drop your raw clips in a folder tell it "cut the filler, tighten the pauses, warm cinematic grade" and let it work And yes, it's a tool, not a taste replacement. it'll hand you a clean, tight cut, but the creative calls, the hook, the pacing, the story.. that's still you. it does the 80% that's grunt work so you spend your time on the 20% that matters. editing stopped being a skill you grind for years. it's a prompt now. repo:

items.title: This site looks like it was built by a team of 3D developers It wasn't. It's just video. And tha
items.description: This site looks like it was built by a team of 3D developers It wasn't. It's just video. And that's the part nobody expects > he generated two frames with AI > turned them into a clip with Seedance 2.0 > then gave it to
items.prompt: This site looks like it was built by a team of 3D developers It wasn't. It's just video. And that's the part nobody expects > he generated two frames with AI > turned them into a clip with Seedance 2.0 > then gave it to Claude 4.8 to wrap into a full immersive scrollable experience Three effects that make the user feel like they're physically moving through a space: > see-through scrub > 3D scroll > mouse tracking None of them are actually 3D. The browser never renders a single polygon. It's a video being controlled by scroll position and cursor movement Most people assume sites like this require a creative developer who specializes in WebGL and weeks of optimization. The actual pipeline is three steps and fits in an afternoon full walkthrough in the article below. bookmark this before you need it

items.description: iPhone camera control for Seedance 2.0 the app for 3D camera and AI motion transfer is now in test flight, link below. You stage the scene and can walk around it and capture real camera movement in 3D space, then Seedanc
items.prompt: iPhone camera control for Seedance 2.0 the app for 3D camera and AI motion transfer is now in test flight, link below. You stage the scene and can walk around it and capture real camera movement in 3D space, then Seedance brings the scene to life. Handheld camera motion and realistic shake is hard to describe or prompt to Al. The iphone's ARKit is super precise with motion tracking and you can record complex scenes as a one man studio. The characters can move and I am working on motion capture. Here is the Al filmmaking workflow: 1. Set the scene in 3D space 2. Record camera motion 3. Generate a starting image 4. Feed image and video to Seedance This was a quick build with claude code but now it is in test flight. Happy if it helps others.

items.title: A 20 YEAR OLD IS MAKING $20,000 A MONTH WITH THE LAZIEST BUSINESS MODEL ON THE INTERNET. SHE WOR
items.description: A 20 YEAR OLD IS MAKING $20,000 A MONTH WITH THE LAZIEST BUSINESS MODEL ON THE INTERNET. SHE WORKS TWENTY MINUTES A DAY FROM HER CAR. SHE HAS NEVER PRODUCED AN ORIGINAL IDEA. Children's YouTube is a goldmine. Channels wi
items.prompt: A 20 YEAR OLD IS MAKING $20,000 A MONTH WITH THE LAZIEST BUSINESS MODEL ON THE INTERNET. SHE WORKS TWENTY MINUTES A DAY FROM HER CAR. SHE HAS NEVER PRODUCED AN ORIGINAL IDEA. Children's YouTube is a goldmine. Channels with simple songs and bright colors pull hundreds of millions of views. Traditional studios spend a fortune to animate them. She spends zero dollars and does it from the driver's seat. She opens YouTube on her phone. She finds a viral baby video with millions of views. She copies the link. She uses a free tool to rip the entire transcript of the video. She takes that transcript and pastes it into an AI platform called Swagga. She types one sentence: "Make me a prompt for a similar video using this as inspiration." The AI acts as her creative director. It analyzes the viral song and spits out a highly detailed master prompt for a new, original 3D animation. She copies the prompt. She switches to the video generator tab. She selects Sora 2 and hits generate. Three minutes later, the AI outputs a fully rendered, broadcast-ready 3D animation. A brightly colored, musical video designed specifically to capture the attention of a toddler. She downloads it. She posts it. She puts her car in drive. The entire process takes twenty minutes. Her channel pulls in thousands of dollars a week in AdSense. She is running a complete children's television network without an office, without animators, and without original ideas. The traditional creator economy is obsessed with authenticity and hard work. The ghost economy is obsessed with leverage. Most people will watch her video and call it lazy. A few will open a new tab.

items.title: On this day in 2023, Elon Musk officially announced xAI. A lot has happened since then… • Jul 12
items.description: On this day in 2023, Elon Musk officially announced xAI. A lot has happened since then… • Jul 12, 2023 — xAI was officially announced with the mission of understanding the true nature of the universe. • Nov 3, 2023 — Unv
items.prompt: On this day in 2023, Elon Musk officially announced xAI. A lot has happened since then… • Jul 12, 2023 — xAI was officially announced with the mission of understanding the true nature of the universe. • Nov 3, 2023 — Unveiled Grok, its first AI model, trained in only two months and connected to real-time knowledge from 𝕏. • Nov 6, 2023 — Launched PromptIDE for prompt engineering and interpretability research. • Mar 17, 2024 — Open-released Grok-1’s weights and architecture, a 314B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model. • Mar 28, 2024 — Announced Grok-1.5 with major reasoning improvements and a 128K-token context window. • Apr 12, 2024 — Introduced Grok-1.5V, xAI’s first multimodal model for understanding images, documents, charts, diagrams and screenshots. • May 26, 2024 — Raised $6 billion in Series B funding. • Aug 13, 2024 — Launched Grok-2 and Grok-2 mini, bringing stronger reasoning, coding, vision and image generation to Grok. • Sep 2024 — Brought Colossus online with 100,000 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, built from start to finish in only 122 days. • Nov 4, 2024 — Opened the xAI API public beta. • Dec 9, 2024 — Launched Aurora, xAI’s own autoregressive image-generation model. • Dec 12, 2024 — Made Grok free for everyone on 𝕏 while adding web search, citations and multilingual improvements. • Dec 23, 2024 — Raised another $6 billion through its Series C funding round. • Jan–Feb 2025 — Launched standalone Grok applications for iOS and Android. • Feb 2025 — Doubled Colossus to 200,000 GPUs just 92 days after completing the first 100,000-GPU cluster. • Feb 19, 2025 — Unveiled Grok 3 and Grok 3 mini, trained with roughly 10× more compute, alongside Think and DeepSearch. • Feb–Mar 2025 — Rolled out Voice Mode and the SuperGrok subscription. • Mar 17, 2025 — Acquired AI video-generation startup Hotshot. • Mar 28, 2025 — xAI acquired 𝕏 in an all-stock transaction, combining data, models, compute, distribution and talent. • Apr–May 2025 — Released Grok 3 through the API and launched Live Search for real-time information from the web and 𝕏. • Jul 9, 2025 — Launched Grok 4 and Grok 4 Heavy with native tool use, real-time search and reinforcement learning at unprecedented scale. • Jul 12, 2025 — Brought Grok directly items.title: Claude Fable running ComfyUI workflows through the Comfy MCP is a cheat code. → Claude pulled th
items.description: Claude Fable running ComfyUI workflows through the Comfy MCP is a cheat code. → Claude pulled the shot from the web, auto-detected the scene cuts + trimmed it → ran my saved depthanything v3 + openpose workflow to build
items.prompt: Claude Fable running ComfyUI workflows through the Comfy MCP is a cheat code. → Claude pulled the shot from the web, auto-detected the scene cuts + trimmed it → ran my saved depthanything v3 + openpose workflow to build the driving reference video → scrubbed every frame for the sharpest one, then character swapped me in with gpt-image-2 exactly how I directed it: wearing a tuxedo, liminal space environment, no background characters → wrote the seedance 2.0 prompts + ran the gens → stitched together the 3-panel comparison you're watching The preprocessing + prompt writing is usually most of the work on a clip like this. With the MCP I didn't touch any of it. Didn't need to look at a single node lol.

items.title: I experimented a lot with the prompt until I landed on this version😎 Only face reference + exac
items.description: I experimented a lot with the prompt until I landed on this version😎 Only face reference + exact 15-second audio. Generated in @higgsfield_ai Seedance 2.0. Small tip: when uploading reference audio, cut it precisely to
items.prompt: I experimented a lot with the prompt until I landed on this version😎 Only face reference + exact 15-second audio. Generated in @higgsfield_ai Seedance 2.0. Small tip: when uploading reference audio, cut it precisely to whole seconds (6, 8, 10, 15…) with no leftover tails 🎵 Prompt: A cinematic 15-second music video shot in a dark modern dance studio with smooth grey reflective floor, black walls and horizontal neon light tubes. Continuous dynamic camera movement, mostly medium shots and close-ups, never too wide. The woman is constantly moving, no freezes or static poses. Main subject: young woman with messy medium-length wavy brown hair with bangs partially covering her face, freckles on nose and cheeks, blue-grey eyes, full glossy lips. She wears a tight black fishnet bodysuit. She is singing the entire time with clear, precise lip-sync, mouth actively moving, intense emotional expression. Lyrics: "Ye! Ye! You keep a box of names / In the drawer by your bed / Polaroids and ticket stubs / Stuffed under the red / You never take one thing / You take the whole last spark / Leave a little thumbprint / On every private heart". 0-2s: Tight medium close-up. She leans her upper body back, head tilting, singing passionately with strong lip-sync, hair falling over her face, body arching, one hand sliding across her chest. 2-4s: Camera slowly pushes in and circles. She comes out of the deep arch, torso still bent forward, hands on her thighs, lifting her head and looking straight into camera while singing with aggressive lip-sync. Three male dancers of different appearances (different ethnicities, hair styles and builds) wearing black tank tops and black wide pants are already close around her, moving with her in low tense postures. The other two men are visible at the edges of the frame, approaching. 4-7s: Medium close-up. She drops lower, body still in constant motion, hair swinging, singing intensely with clear mouth movements, sharp head turns, eyes locked on camera. Male dancers stay close, their hands lightly touching her as they move together. 8-12s: Medium shot with slow camera drift. Exactly five male dancers of completely different appearances, all wearing black tank tops and black wide pants, surround her tightly on the floor in a dense, intertwined formation. She is in the center, body still moving, upper body rising and shifting, singing with strong lip-sync. All five men move subtly with her, never static. The men never fully obscure her body. 13-15s: Dynamic medium shot. The five diverse male dancers lift her into the air in a powerful deep backbend. Her body is fully extended and arched, head thrown back, still singing with clear lip-sync. While holding her they gently rock her up and down in time with the beat. The camera starts from a clear side view of her arched body and smoothly transitions to a frontal view of her face. At the end they lower her smoothly onto her feet; she lands and immediately continues singing as the five men stay low on the floor around her. The men never fully obscure her. High fashion dance energy, sweaty skin, sharp timing, continuous fluid motion of the woman, priority on accurate lip-sync in every frame. Rules: no hand morphing, no body distortions, clean stable anatomy, fingers and hands remain consistent and natural throughout the entire video. #AIvideo #AIMusicVideo #AIFilmmaking

items.title: A 21-year-old runs a $15,200/month children’s animation studio from a room above her parents’ la
items.description: A 21-year-old runs a $15,200/month children’s animation studio from a room above her parents’ laundromat. She has no animators, cannot draw, and has never owned a camera. Her entire production stack costs $68/month. She
items.prompt: A 21-year-old runs a $15,200/month children’s animation studio from a room above her parents’ laundromat. She has no animators, cannot draw, and has never owned a camera. Her entire production stack costs $68/month. She uses Claude to study the structure behind successful children’s channels: recurring stories, colors, pacing, thumbnails, and the moments that keep toddlers watching. It then writes original episodes around Milo, a small bear who always wears yellow pajamas. An AI video generator turns each script into a finished cartoon in 11 minutes. She reviews the episode on her phone, approves it, and adds it to a library that now contains 186 videos. While washing machines vibrate through the floor, Milo explores forests, builds toy houses, and teaches children new words. Her analytics show 3.2 million views. Her PayPal shows $6,350 in recent deposits. The channel now averages $15,200 in monthly profit. She also films tutorials explaining how the workflow works. Those videos attract adults interested in AI. But the tutorials are not the business. The tutorials get attention. Milo builds the library, collects the watch time, and turns one character into a media asset she completely controls. A traditional studio would hire a team before creating 186 episodes. She created Milo first and let AI become the team.

items.title: Do you realize what the hell just happened? Fable 5 just flipped the entire website-building gam
items.description: Do you realize what the hell just happened? Fable 5 just flipped the entire website-building game right in front of you. Fully animated, studio-looking sites, smooth scrolling, 3D globes, video backgrounds, all mobile re
items.prompt: Do you realize what the hell just happened? Fable 5 just flipped the entire website-building game right in front of you. Fully animated, studio-looking sites, smooth scrolling, 3D globes, video backgrounds, all mobile responsive, built in one session by describing what you want. No agency, no code, no months of work. Here's the workflow, start to finish: 1. Find a look on Pinterest. Grab an image with the vibe you want (3D, animation, depth). 2. Turn it into a video. Use an AI image-to-video tool (Higgsfield, Kling) to animate it. Tell it to move but not zoom or reposition. 3. Build the hero with Fable 5. In Claude Code, describe the hero section, drop your video in as the background, set your colors. 4. Build section by section. Ask for the next sections in the same style. Once Fable has your fonts, colors, and layout locked, it reuses them and avoids generic AI-slop pages. 5. Refine with references. Screenshot a background, blur it in Figma to grab the average color, and tell Fable to match it. 6. Post it to grow. Screen-record the site, post the clip, and tag the tools you used. Companies repost work that shows off their platform. The people building real things with Fable now are going to be way ahead when everyone else catches up. Bookmark this.

items.prompt: Building AI Agents from Scratch Tutorial Series (AI Era Edition) in 30 days.( If interested say hi 👋) Master modern AI agent engineering by building production-ready autonomous AI systems from the ground up. 1. Day 1: What Are AI Agents? – LLMs, Agents, Workflows, and Autonomous Systems Project: Build your first AI chatbot agent. 2. Day 2: Setting Up the Development Environment – Python, VS Code, APIs, and Virtual Environments Project: Configure a complete AI agent development workspace. 3. Day 3: Understanding Large Language Models (LLMs) – Prompting, Tokens, Context Windows, and Reasoning Project: Connect your application to an LLM API. 4. Day 4: Prompt Engineering for AI Agents – System Prompts, Instructions, and Few-Shot Learning Project: Build a prompt-driven coding assistant. 5. Day 5: Agent Architecture – Planning, Reasoning, Memory, and Actions Project: Design a reusable AI agent framework. 6. Day 6: Building an Agent Loop – Observe, Think, Plan, Act, and Reflect Project: Create an autonomous task execution agent. 7. Day 7: Tool Calling and Function Calling Project: Build an AI agent that uses weather and calculator tools. 8. Day 8: Memory Systems – Short-Term, Long-Term, and Vector Memory Project: Create an AI assistant that remembers previous conversations. 9. Day 9: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Fundamentals Project: Build a PDF question-answering agent. 10. Day 10: Embeddings and Vector Databases – FAISS, ChromaDB, and Pinecone Project: Create a searchable knowledge base. 11. Day 11: AI Agent Planning Strategies – ReAct, Chain-of-Thought, and Tree of Thoughts Project: Build a reasoning-based planning agent. 12. Day 12: Multi-Step Workflows and Task Decomposition Project: Build an AI research assistant. 13. Day 13: AI Agents with MCP (Model Context Protocol) Project: Connect an AI agent to external developer tools using MCP. 14. Day 14: AI Agents with LangChain Project: Build a document analysis agent. 15. Day 15: AI Agents with LangGraph Project: Build a stateful multi-step AI workflow. 16. Day 16: AI Agents with OpenAI Agents SDK Project: Create a customer support AI agent. 17. Day 17: Browser Automation Agents Project: Build an AI web automation assistant. 18. Day 18: Computer Use Agents Project: Create an AI agent that automates desktop tasks. 19. Day 19: Coding Agents and Software Engineering Assistants Project: Build an AI code review assistant. 20. Day 20: AI Agents with APIs and Databases Project: Build an AI-powered CRM assistant. 21. Day 21: Multi-Agent Systems – Collaboration and Communication Project: Build a team of specialized AI agents. 22. Day 22: AI Agent Security, Guardrails, and Safety Project: Secure an AI agent against prompt injection. 23. Day 23: AI Agent Evaluation and Benchmarking Project: Build an automated testing framework for agents. 24. Day 24: Voice AI Agents – Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech Project: Build a voice assistant. 25. Day 25: Vision AI Agents – Image Understanding and Multimodal Models Project: Build an AI image analysis assistant. 26. Day 26: AI Agents with Local Models – Ollama, LM Studio, and Open-Source LLMs Project: Run an offline AI agent locally. 27. Day 27: Deploying AI Agents – Docker, Cloud, CI/CD, and Monitoring Project: Deploy a production-ready AI agent. 28. Day 28: AI SaaS Architecture – Authentication, Billing, and User Management Project: Build an AI Agent SaaS platform. 29. Day 29: Scaling AI Agents – Performance, Cost Optimization, Caching, and Observability Project: Optimize an enterprise AI agent deployment. 30. Day 30: Final Capstone Project – Build a Production-Ready AI Agent Platform with Memory, RAG, MCP, Tool Calling, Multi-Agent Collaboration, Authentication, Docker Deployment, Monitoring, and Cloud Hosting. Grab the Building AI Agents from Scratch Ebook:  #AIAgents #AgenticAI #LLM #ArtificialIntelligence Follow @e_opore to learn more.

items.prompt: Day 10/20 in creating AI videos for my portfolio Today's video is for Orchid&Ash, a US-based skincare brand. How I did it: • Fed my idea to Claude and created the prompt for a 7-scene storyboard for the Restorative Face cream • Created the storyboard image with ChatGPT • Turned the frames into 4-second videos using Google flow (used the 9:16 framing) • Produced the voice-over in Eleven Labs, using an American female voice. • Edited the pacing and sound in CapCut What do you think about it? items.title: Cooking meets cinematic AI. This stunning commercial was created with Seedance 2.0 in @LartAI ..
items.description: Cooking meets cinematic AI. This stunning commercial was created with Seedance 2.0 in @Lart_AI Delivering realistic motion, rich food details, and premium visuals that look like a real production. Create yours here 👇  P
items.prompt: Cooking meets cinematic AI. This stunning commercial was created with Seedance 2.0 in @Lart_AI Delivering realistic motion, rich food details, and premium visuals that look like a real production. Create yours here 👇  Prompt: THE FLAME CHEF - 15 second high-energy cinematic cooking film, 10 rapid scenes, fast rhythmic pacing, whip-pan and speed-ramp transitions between every scene, kinetic kitchen artistry from fresh ingredients to final plated dish. Shot on ARRI Alexa 35, 35mm lens, natural film grain, rich warm color grade with deep shadows and golden highlights, professional food cinematography, photorealistic, not CGI. Dark moody restaurant kitchen, dramatic overhead lighting, steam and warm glow. SCENE 1 (0-1.5s): Chef's hand places a cast iron pan onto a glowing gas burner with confident energy, blue flame rising around the edges, quick real time then micro slow motion on the flame glow, hard whip-pan into locked close-up. SCENE 2 (1.5-3s): Rapid skilled vegetable prep, knife rocking smoothly through fresh herbs and bell peppers in rhythmic motion, hyper-fast real time with hands in controlled motion, one pepper slice spinning in brief slow motion, top-down locked shot. SCENE 3 (3-4.5s): Butter cube tossed gracefully through the air landing in the warm pan, melting instantly into golden foam and swirl, slow motion flight with speed ramp to real time on the sizzle, side macro tracking the arc. SCENE 4 (4.5-6s): Chef flips the pan, vegetables rising upward in a golden arc, warm flame glow beneath, tiny seasoning embers drifting, speed ramp from real time flip into dramatic slow motion at the peak of the toss, low hero angle. SCENE 5 (6-7.5s): Extreme macro, garlic and chili meeting hot oil, lively golden bubbles, steam rising in a soft backlit plume, 120fps slow motion with every bubble crisp, probe lens push-in through the steam. SCENE 6 (7.5-9s): Chef seasoning from height with flowing hand movement, salt crystals raining down in slow motion through a shaft of overhead light catching like snow, slow motion rain against fast hand movement, side close-up with shallow focus on the crystals. SCENE 7 (9-10.5s): Sauce poured from a steel pan in a glossy ribbon coating the dish in one continuous silky wave, steam curling, silky hypnotic slow motion, orbiting macro around the pour. SCENE 8 (10.5-12s): Fresh herbs dropped from above, leaves tumbling in slow motion landing perfectly on the glistening dish, tiny sparkle of sauce, slow motion fall with real time landing, top-down locked shot. SCENE 9 (12-13.5s): Chef wipes the plate rim in one confident swift motion and spins the plate a quarter turn, steam rising through dramatic side light, fast confident real time, close-up tracking the hand with whip-pan on the spin. SCENE 10 (13.5-15s): Final hero shot, finished dish center frame under a single overhead spotlight, steam rising in elegant curls, background fading to darkness, chef's silhouette stepping back with satisfaction, calm slow motion after the energy, slow push-in settling to a loop-friendly hold. Movement: continuous kinetic energy throughout, hands moving with professional speed and precision, constant speed ramping between hyper-fast real time and crisp slow motion for rhythm, ingredients gracefully in flight, intensity building from scene 1 to 9 then sudden calm hero ending Camera: hard whip-pans between scenes, top-down locked shots, side macro tracking, low hero angle, probe lens push-in, orbiting pour shot, close-up hand tracking, slow push-in finale, all cuts punchy and landing on beat Effects: warm flame glow, backlit steam plumes, golden oil bubbles in extreme macro, salt crystals in overhead light shafts, glossy sauce reflections, shallow depth of field, natural film grain, dark moody kitchen atmosphere, no text no watermark.

SCENE 2 (1.5-3s): Rapid skilled vegetable prep, knife rocking smoothly through fresh herbs and bell peppers in rhythmic motion, hyper-fast real time with hands in controlled motion, one pepper slice spinning in brief slow motion, top-down locked shot. SCENE 3 (3-4.5s): Butter cube tossed gracefully through the air landing in the warm pan, melting instantly into golden foam and swirl, slow motion flight with speed ramp to real time on the sizzle, side macro tracking the arc. SCENE 4 (4.5-6s): Chef flips the pan, vegetables rising upward in a golden arc, warm flame glow beneath, tiny seasoning embers drifting, speed ramp from real time flip into dramatic slow motion at the peak of the toss, low hero angle. SCENE 5 (6-7.5s): Extreme macro, garlic and chili meeting hot oil, lively golden bubbles, steam rising in a soft backlit plume, 120fps slow motion with every bubble crisp, probe lens push-in through the steam. SCENE 6 (7.5-9s): Chef seasoning from height with flowing hand movement, salt crystals raining down in slow motion through a shaft of overhead light catching like snow, slow motion rain against fast hand movement, side close-up with shallow focus on the crystals. SCENE 7 (9-10.5s): Sauce poured from a steel pan in a glossy ribbon coating the dish in one continuous silky wave, steam curling, silky hypnotic slow motion, orbiting macro around the pour. SCENE 8 (10.5-12s): Fresh herbs dropped from above, leaves tumbling in slow motion landing perfectly on the glistening dish, tiny sparkle of sauce, slow motion fall with real time landing, top-down locked shot. SCENE 9 (12-13.5s): Chef wipes the plate rim in one confident swift motion and spins the plate a quarter turn, steam rising through dramatic side light, fast confident real time, close-up tracking the hand with whip-pan on the spin. SCENE 10 (13.5-15s): Final hero shot, finished dish center frame under a single overhead spotlight, steam rising in elegant curls, background fading to darkness, chef's silhouette stepping back with satisfaction, calm slow motion after the energy, slow push-in settling to a loop-friendly hold. Movement: continuous kinetic energy throughout, hands moving with professional speed and precision, constant speed ramping between hyper-fast real time and crisp slow motion for rhythm, ingredients gracefully in flight, intensity building from scene 1 to 9 then sudden calm hero ending Camera: hard whip-pans between scenes, top-down locked shots, side macro tracking, low hero angle, probe lens push-in, orbiting pour shot, close-up hand tracking, slow push-in finale, all cuts punchy and landing on beat Effects: warm flame glow, backlit steam plumes, golden oil bubbles in extreme macro, salt crystals in overhead light shafts, glossy sauce reflections, shallow depth of field, natural film grain, dark moody kitchen atmosphere, no text no watermark.

items.description: Seedance 2.0 Walking Home in the Rain, created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject: Young Korean woman, early 20s. Natural appearance. Black wavy hair loosely tied into a slightly messy ponytail with wispy bangs. Lig
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 Walking Home in the Rain, created using Seedance 2.0 Prompt: Main Subject: Young Korean woman, early 20s. Natural appearance. Black wavy hair loosely tied into a slightly messy ponytail with wispy bangs. Light grey zip hoodie worn open over a faded white tank top. Relaxed blue jeans. Black canvas sneakers. Natural skin texture, minimal makeup, ordinary everyday look. Consistent appearance throughout the video. Location: Authentic Korean residential neighborhood during a light summer rain shower. Narrow concrete alleys, low-rise houses, small gardens, potted plants, bicycles, utility poles, overhead cables, wet pavement, puddles, rain-darkened walls, leafy trees. Camera Style Early-2000s consumer DV camcorder. Friend casually filming. Heavy handheld shake. Imperfect framing. Subject frequently drifts toward frame edges. Late reframing. Autofocus hunting. Lens breathing. Exposure pumping. Motion blur. Rolling shutter. Digital compression artifacts. Slight sensor noise. Soft detail. Faded colors. Low contrast. Occasional blown highlights. No stabilization. No cinematic movements. No modern color grading. The camera operator behaves like a real friend with no filmmaking experience. 00:00–00:02 Standing beneath a small apartment awning while rain falls across the street. She leans forward and extends her hand into the rain to test how heavy it is. A passing vehicle somewhere off-frame splashes water nearby. She immediately laughs and pulls her hand back. The camera misses part of the reaction because the operator reframes too slowly. Rain droplets briefly appear on the lens. Autofocus hunts between her face and the rainy street. 00:02–00:04 She steps into the drizzle and starts walking down the narrow alley. The camera follows unevenly from behind. Her sneakers splash through shallow puddles. Wet strands of hair begin sticking to her cheek and neck. Wind moves her hoodie slightly. The operator almost loses her from frame while turning a corner. 00:04–00:06 Passing a row of potted plants outside a house. She casually brushes rainwater from a large leaf. The camera unexpectedly focuses on the leaf instead of her face. Exposure shifts as the scene moves from open sky into tree shade. She continues walking before camera catches up. 00:06–00:08 A neighborhood cat shelters beneath a parked bicycle. She crouches down briefly and says a few friendly words. The cat sniffs her hand. Camera focus repeatedly jumps between the bicycle wheel, the cat, and her face. One moment is partially blocked by the operator's movement. 00:08–00:10 Walking again. Rainwater drips from overhead branches onto her shoulder. She reacts naturally and laughs to herself. The camera is slightly too close and awkwardly reframes. A gust of wind moves nearby leaves and loose hair strands. 00:10–00:12 Near a small intersection. She pauses while waiting for a scooter to pass in the distance. The camera drifts toward the wet road before finding her again. She wipes rain from her forehead using her sleeve. Her clothing shows subtle dampness from the weather. 00:12–00:15 Walking away down the rain-darkened street. The camera trails several steps behind. She suddenly realizes she is still being filmed. She glances back over her shoulder. A small genuine smile appears. She looks away again and keeps walking. The operator lowers the camcorder slightly. Recording abruptly cuts to black mid-motion as if the camera was accidentally switched off. Audio (Natural only) Light rainfall. Water dripping from roofs and leaves. Footsteps on wet concrete. Distant neighborhood traffic. Occasional scooter passing far away. Birds sheltering in trees. Rustling leaves. Faint residential chatter. Cat sounds. Subtle wind. No music. No narration. No sound design. No added effects. Generation Priority Authenticity > Beauty Believability > Cinematics Natural Imperfections > Technical Perfection The final result should look indistinguishable from a real early-2000s camcorder scene.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 is pretty wild 100% AI What if Jurassic Park was real? PROMPT: Format: Found-footag
items.description: Seedance 2.0 is pretty wild 100% AI What if Jurassic Park was real? PROMPT: Format: Found-footage vacation video, 15 seconds, seven shots with hard cuts — a tourist's camera roll from one full day, morning to dusk. Image
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 is pretty wild 100% AI What if Jurassic Park was real? PROMPT: Format: Found-footage vacation video, 15 seconds, seven shots with hard cuts — a tourist's camera roll from one full day, morning to dusk. Image reference: Use the attached gate photo as the exact visual reference for Shot 1 — match its architecture, proportions, torch placement, lighting, and signage precisely. Main subject: Young woman, blond hair in a loose low bun shedding strands, oversized faded olive t-shirt, denim shorts, white canvas sneakers, disposable-camera strap on her wrist. Realistic skin texture, minimal makeup, wide-eyed tourist joy. Maintain perfect visual consistency — identity, hair, clothing, proportions — across every shot and location. Location: A vast island dinosaur preserve. Jungle mountains, open savanna paddocks, wooden observation structures, tall electrified perimeter fences, dirt tour roads. Dinosaurs are photorealistic animals — muted natural hides, birdlike motion, visible breathing weight. Filmed like wildlife, never like monsters. Camera style: Mid-range smartphone, handheld by her friend. Heavy shake, autofocus hunting, motion blur on pans, compression grain, exposure pumping. Each cut is abrupt and mid-motion, like real unedited camera-roll clips. 00:00–00:03 — THE GATES (morning, filmed from inside a moving open-top tour jeep). The camera films forward over the seats as the jeep approaches the colossal timber gates from the reference image — flared stone-and-wood towers, flaming torches, jungle pressing in on both sides, the road still wet from morning rain. The doors groan open. She spins around from the front seat into frame, gripping the roll bar, and half-shouts over the engine: "Oh my god, this is wild! I can't believe we're actually here!" — as the jeep passes under the archway into shadow and out again. 00:03–00:05 — SAVANNA (late morning, wide shot from a raised wooden platform). She stands small at the rail against an open plain where a herd of long-necked giants wades through heat haze, one rearing slowly against the treeline. The camera zooms in shakily, overshoots, corrects. 00:05–00:07 — RIVER CROSSING (midday, low angle through the jeep's open side). Past her shoulder as the jeep fords a shallow river: a crested duck-billed dinosaur drinks twenty meters away, lifting its head to watch them pass, water dripping from its jaw. She raises her disposable camera; the phone catches its tiny click. 00:07–00:09 — JUNGLE FENCE (afternoon, handheld walking shot). Dim under the canopy beside a tall humming fence. She peers through the wire — and a small crested predator peers back from the ferns, head tilting in quick birdlike jerks. She backs up a slow half-step, whispering "okay... hi." Autofocus argues between wire and eye. 00:09–00:11 — THE HERD RUNS (late afternoon, chaotic pan from the jeep at speed). A flock of small striped dinosaurs floods across and around the vehicle. The camera whips left-right, blurred bodies streaking past, her whooping off-mic, dust on the lens. 00:11–00:13 — QUIET GIANT (golden hour, static shot at a wooden overlook). The frame finally settles: silhouetted against low sun, she stands at a rail as an immense horned dinosaur grazes just below, close enough to hear grass tearing. She isn't laughing anymore — just watching. 00:13–00:15 — DUSK ROAR (blue hour, half-framed and accidental). Filmed as the phone was being lowered, frame tilted: a colossal, deep bellow rolls in from the darkening jungle far off. Everyone freezes and turns toward it. Cut to black mid-turn. Audio: Ambient only, shifting per location — jeep engine and gate groan, her excited line over the motor, savanna wind, river wading, fence hum, thundering small feet, grass tearing, and the single distant bellow that swallows everything. No music. No narration. Style & quality boosters: Raw consumer camera-roll aesthetic, abrupt mid-motion cuts, heavy handheld instability, natural motion blur, golden-hour lens flare, coherent animal mass and physics — ground tremor, water displacement, dust. No text overlays, no watermarks. Goal: One unforgettable day inside the world's most famous dinosaur preserve, told through seven imperfect tourist clips — opening on the gates everyone recognizes, and closing on the sound that reminds them what lives beyond the fences.

items.title: Higgsfield has now open-sourced its Originals projects, which means you can see the exact prompt
items.description: Higgsfield has now open-sourced its Originals projects, which means you can see the exact prompts, references, assets, and generation settings behind the final shots.. Love that Higgsfield is making the workflow transpar
items.prompt: Higgsfield has now open-sourced its Originals projects, which means you can see the exact prompts, references, assets, and generation settings behind the final shots.. Love that Higgsfield is making the workflow transparent so creators can study, recreate, and remix. Full open-sourced prompts & assets below: Seedance 2.0 on Higgsfield AI @Image1 is the LOGO REFERENCE — neon lime-green rounded square with a black flowing squiggle ribbon mark. It replaces Benjamin Franklin's portrait on every dollar bill: engraving-style hundreds, squiggle in the portrait oval, lime-green tinted accents. @Image2 is the LOCATION REFERENCE — this exact NYC brownstone street at golden hour; match palette, light and lens texture precisely. Scene: insert scene of one continuous music video, no lipsync, no dialogue. VARIANT E — AFTERMATH POETRY: the jackpot already happened — we open mid-storm at its PEAK and ride it down to a beautiful quiet ending. Neighborhood kids (Black and brown, 8-14, puffers, hoodies, durags, one with a skateboard) everywhere. COMPOSITION — CRAZY ANGLES, NOTHING CENTERED: 0–2s COLD OPEN at maximum chaos: extreme HIGH ANGLE from rooftop height straight down — the sidewalk is a snow-globe of spinning bills, kids swirling in van-Gogh spirals below, the ATM still coughing bursts in the lower-left corner. 2–4.5s inverted low-angle looking straight UP from the pavement: bills silhouetted against the golden sky between cornices like a flock of birds, kids' jumping bodies eclipsing the sun flare, dutch rotation slowly twisting the horizon. 4.5–8s ULTRA SLOW MOTION: the film's signature bill drifts past the camera at eyeball distance, rotating end-over-end — squiggle logo crisp in the portrait oval, lime tint glowing, engraving lines shimmering; behind it the chaos softens into golden bokeh; a small hand reaches into frame and JUST misses it — SNAP back to speed. 8–11s the storm thins: gliding steadicam at knee height through settling paper, kids kneeling to sweep bills into caps and hoods, one kid lying flat on his back on the sidewalk making a 'snow angel' in the drift of bills, laughing at the sky. 11–15s the quiet button: slow push-in at pavement level — the skateboard rolls through frame right-to-left with three bills stacked on its deck like cargo; behind it in soft focus the last kids drift home down the street's diagonal; a final bill flips once in the breeze and settles face-up on the lower-right third, squiggle logo up, long shadows stretching, flare dying in the upper-left. Audio: NO music diegetic only: paper storm settling to soft flutter, kids' laughter fading down the block, skateboard wheels on concrete, a breeze, distant city evening. Quality bar: expensive cinematic insert with a poetic ending, Kodak texture matching @Image2, real paper physics, squiggle the only readable mark, no AI gloss. @higgsfield_ai

items.rawText: Higgsfield has now open-sourced its Originals projects, which means you can see the exact prompts, references, assets, and generation settings behind the final shots.. Love that Higgsfield is making the workflow transparent so creators can study, recreate, and remix. Full open-sourced prompts & assets below: Seedance 2.0 on Higgsfield AI @Image1 is the LOGO REFERENCE — neon lime-green rounded square with a black flowing squiggle ribbon mark. It replaces Benjamin Franklin's portrait on every dollar bill: engraving-style hundreds, squiggle in the portrait oval, lime-green tinted accents. @Image2 is the LOCATION REFERENCE — this exact NYC brownstone street at golden hour; match palette, light and lens texture precisely. Scene: insert scene of one continuous music video, no lipsync, no dialogue. VARIANT E — AFTERMATH POETRY: the jackpot already happened — we open mid-storm at its PEAK and ride it down to a beautiful quiet ending. Neighborhood kids (Black and brown, 8-14, puffers, hoodies, durags, one with a skateboard) everywhere. COMPOSITION — CRAZY ANGLES, NOTHING CENTERED: 0–2s COLD OPEN at maximum chaos: extreme HIGH ANGLE from rooftop height straight down — the sidewalk is a snow-globe of spinning bills, kids swirling in van-Gogh spirals below, the ATM still coughing bursts in the lower-left corner. 2–4.5s inverted low-angle looking straight UP from the pavement: bills silhouetted against the golden sky between cornices like a flock of birds, kids' jumping bodies eclipsing the sun flare, dutch rotation slowly twisting the horizon. 4.5–8s ULTRA SLOW MOTION: the film's signature bill drifts past the camera at eyeball distance, rotating end-over-end — squiggle logo crisp in the portrait oval, lime tint glowing, engraving lines shimmering; behind it the chaos softens into golden bokeh; a small hand reaches into frame and JUST misses it — SNAP back to speed. 8–11s the storm thins: gliding steadicam at knee height through settling paper, kids kneeling to sweep bills into caps and hoods, one kid lying flat on his back on the sidewalk making a 'snow angel' in the drift of bills, laughing at the sky. 11–15s the quiet button: slow push-in at pavement level — the skateboard rolls through frame right-to-left with three bills stacked on its deck like cargo; behind it in soft focus the last kids drift home down the street's diagonal; a final bill flips once in the breeze and settles face-up on the lower-right third, squiggle logo up, long shadows stretching, flare dying in the upper-left. Audio: NO music diegetic only: paper storm settling to soft flutter, kids' laughter fading down the block, skateboard wheels on concrete, a breeze, distant city evening. Quality bar: expensive cinematic insert with a poetic ending, Kodak texture matching @Image2, real paper physics, squiggle the only readable mark, no AI gloss. @higgsfield_ai

items.title: THERE'S A SECOND SKILL EVERY GREAT AI VIDEO CREATOR HAS — AND ALMOST NOBODY TALKS ABOUT IT Chara
items.description: THERE'S A SECOND SKILL EVERY GREAT AI VIDEO CREATOR HAS — AND ALMOST NOBODY TALKS ABOUT IT Character consistency gets all the attention. Nobody mentions the other half of the problem — the one that makes a perfectly cons
items.prompt: THERE'S A SECOND SKILL EVERY GREAT AI VIDEO CREATOR HAS — AND ALMOST NOBODY TALKS ABOUT IT Character consistency gets all the attention. Nobody mentions the other half of the problem — the one that makes a perfectly consistent character still look like a slideshow instead of a scene. The secret is camera vocabulary. The creators whose sequences actually feel directed aren't writing better character prompts than you. They're issuing real camera instructions instead of vague ones — and most people never learn the difference. There's a free reference that makes this fast to fix: aicameramovements,com It's not a generation tool, it's a vocabulary bank — every camera move broken down into name, description, speed, framing, and exactly how the shot should end. Whip pan left, tilt up, crash zoom out, dolly in, truck right — each one with a ready-to-copy prompt attached. What actually changes once you use real terms instead of vague ones: - "Camera moves closer" becomes "dolly in — smooth controlled push, subject position consistent while distance closes, finish in a tighter composition" - "Fast cut to wide" becomes "crash zoom out — snap the lens rapidly away, land on a bold wider composition" - Seedance stops guessing your intent because you're no longer describing a feeling, you're issuing a direction Why this matters for anything built with a character sheet or grid: - A locked character still reads as static if every shot uses the same vague "camera moves" instruction - Real shot vocabulary is what turns a consistent character into a directed sequence, not just a slideshow of correct faces - Once you know the terms, you can assign a specific move to each panel in your storyboard instead of leaving pacing to chance If your sequences hold together but still feel flat, this is probably why. It was never just the character sheet. It's the camera language you pair it with. (h/t @NexlowX for the original find)

items.title: POV: The kiss that broke the internet, recreated with AI on the tip of Tokyo Tower. A viral cine
items.description: POV: The kiss that broke the internet, recreated with AI on the tip of Tokyo Tower. A viral cinematic moment, reimagined with seamless camera movement, perfect character consistency, and a breathtaking skyline that never
items.prompt: POV: The kiss that broke the internet, recreated with AI on the tip of Tokyo Tower. A viral cinematic moment, reimagined with seamless camera movement, perfect character consistency, and a breathtaking skyline that never leaves the frame. Every shot stays locked to the very top of Tokyo Tower for a romance that feels straight out of a blockbuster. Made with GPT Image 2 and Seedance on @itsPolloAI for the Peak Moment Challenge. Prompt: PART 1 • 0:00–0:15 — THE REVEAL & THE KISS (9:16) 0:00–0:03 — Arrival Continuous crane arc already in motion around the couple on the tip of Tokyo Tower's antenna mast. Only one Tokyo Tower visible. Wind moves Jessy's hair and trench dress. He wears the yellow kawaii mascot helmet. Jessy: "You're seriously wearing that up here?" Man: "Only for you." 0:03–0:06 — Reveal Camera continues the same arc. He removes the helmet, revealing tousled brown hair and blue eyes. 0:06–0:09 — Pull Continuous crane widens. He pulls Jessy close by the waist, she grabs his jacket collar. The Polloai flag becomes visible above. 0:09–0:12 — Kiss Camera keeps pulling back with a subtle Dutch roll. They kiss on the tip of Tokyo Tower as the grade transitions from hazy news footage to warm cinematic. 0:12–0:15 — Sway Wide shot. They slowly turn while kissing. Tokyo Tower and the Polloai flag remain clearly visible. PART 2 • 0:15–0:25 — THE SPIN & THE VIEW (9:16) Continuity: First frame must exactly match the last frame of Part 1. 0:15–0:18 — Spin Continue the identical crane arc and orbit. He spins Jessy once on the tower tip. Dress, jewelry and blue-streaked hair flow naturally. Jessy: "Don't drop me from up here!" 0:18–0:21 — Second Kiss Orbit tightens into a close two-shot. They share another soft kiss, foreheads touching between kisses. 0:21–0:23 — View Camera cranes upward and pulls back. They face the glowing Tokyo skyline together with the Polloai flag above. 0:23–0:25 — Hold Slow push-in. They remain embracing on the tip of Tokyo Tower until fade to black. Strict: Maintain the exact reference camera movement throughout. All actions happen only on the tip of Tokyo Tower, with only one Tokyo Tower visible in every shot. #PolloAI #PolloPeakMoment

items.title: SHE TOOK ONE SELFIE. AI TURNED IT INTO AN INFINITE PHOTOSHOOT. The trick isn't the image generat
items.description: SHE TOOK ONE SELFIE. AI TURNED IT INTO AN INFINITE PHOTOSHOOT. The trick isn't the image generator. It's one sentence almost nobody uses: "Break this down into JSON." Here's the workflow. You give AI a single photo, and
items.prompt: SHE TOOK ONE SELFIE. AI TURNED IT INTO AN INFINITE PHOTOSHOOT. The trick isn't the image generator. It's one sentence almost nobody uses: "Break this down into JSON." Here's the workflow. You give AI a single photo, and instead of asking for edits, you ask it to describe every visual element as structured code. The lighting. The pose. The face geometry. The exact skin tone. The composition. All of it — captured as a precise JSON blueprint. That blueprint becomes her digital DNA. Now type: "Show her in the back of a Rolls Royce in Dubai at night, scrolling her phone, flash on." Same face. Same person. New world. "Show her in a podcast studio." Done. Perfectly consistent. The craziest part? Character consistency was THE unsolved problem of AI images. Faces drifted. Every generation looked like a different cousin. JSON fixed it — because the model stops guessing what she looks like and starts reading the spec. One selfie becomes a Dubai lifestyle shoot, a podcast appearance, a brand campaign. Influencers pay photographers thousands for what this workflow does in minutes. The people who figured this out aren't booking photoshoots anymore. They're generating them. Follow @Sancho_Wizard for more AI deep dives. Don't forget to bookmark this post for later.

items.title: How to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation all directed from one ch
items.description: How to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation all directed from one chat with the hashtag Comfy MCP. @hellorob didn't let the agent improvise a pipeline. He pointed it at his existing ComfyUI wo
items.prompt: How to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation all directed from one chat with the hashtag Comfy MCP. @hellorob didn't let the agent improvise a pipeline. He pointed it at his existing ComfyUI workflows ( listed below ) and directed every shot: the sprite close-up, the bezel turn, the display flip, and the display changing to the time 10:04. The consistency secret is the driving video: → DepthAnything V3 pass blended with Canny edge lines — fine detail like the tiny debossed logo survives every gen → The original sprite outline kept leaking through those edge lines, so Claude suggested a SAM3 mask over the screen to kill it → With the screen masked, swapping in a new star sprite is just prompting: one GPT-Image-2 reference still + one line in the Seedance prompt And the entire process is now a reusable Claude skill.

items.title: GPT Image 2 + Seedance 2.0 on OpenArt AI Prompt: Create one professional commercial storyboard p
items.description: GPT Image 2 + Seedance 2.0 on OpenArt AI Prompt: Create one professional commercial storyboard presentation showing 10 cinematic frames arranged in a clean 2×5 grid on a white presentation board. Thin black borders separ
items.prompt: GPT Image 2 + Seedance 2.0 on OpenArt AI Prompt: Create one professional commercial storyboard presentation showing 10 cinematic frames arranged in a clean 2×5 grid on a white presentation board. Thin black borders separate each frame. Each panel contains a bold scene number (01–10), timestamp, and a short production caption underneath. The storyboard should look like a real advertising agency pre-production board for a luxury chocolate commercial. TITLE KITKAT "The Art of the Snap" Luxury Commercial Storyboard • 15 Seconds STYLE Inspired by the cinematic luxury aesthetic of premium chocolate commercials. Rich dark background, dramatic lighting, elegant slow motion, sensual textures, premium food photography, macro cinematography, high-end advertising, ultra photorealistic. SCENE 01 (0.0–1.5s) Complete darkness. A single KitKat bar slowly emerges from shadow onto a polished dark stone pedestal. Tiny cocoa dust floats through warm golden light. Caption: The Reveal SCENE 02 (1.5–3.0s) Extreme macro. Camera glides across the embossed KitKat logo. Chocolate surface reflects soft warm highlights. Every texture is perfectly visible. Caption: Luxury Detail SCENE 03 (3.0–4.5s) Roasted cocoa beans. Madagascar vanilla pods. Fresh milk splash. Wafer layers. Chocolate curls. Everything slowly floats around the chocolate bar. Caption: Ingredients Rise SCENE 04 (4.5–6.0s) The KitKat bar snaps perfectly in half. Time almost freezes. Chocolate shards. Wafer crumbs. Cocoa powder. Chocolate flakes. Explode outward in beautiful slow motion. Caption: The Perfect Snap SCENE 05 (6.0–7.5s) Camera performs a slow cinematic orbit around the broken KitKat pieces. The creamy wafer layers are exposed. Chocolate dust dances through the light. Caption: Floating Luxury SCENE 06 (7.5–9.0s) Extreme macro. Molten chocolate slowly pours over the broken wafer. The chocolate moves like silk. Tiny reflections shimmer. Caption: Chocolate Flow SCENE 07 (9.0–10.5s) A cascade of liquid chocolate envelops the KitKat. Chocolate ribbons twist elegantly. Cocoa particles sparkle. Caption: Pure Indulgence SCENE 08 (10.5–12.0s) Everything freezes. Chocolate droplets. Wafer crumbs. Chocolate curls. Cocoa powder. All suspended perfectly around the product. Caption: Frozen Perfection SCENE 09 (12.0–13.5s) Ultimate hero shot. Complete KitKat package standing beside the broken chocolate bar. Warm cinematic spotlight. Premium luxury composition. Caption: Hero Product SCENE 10 (13.5–15.0s) Minimal luxury background. The KitKat logo appears. Elegant typography fades in. Tagline: "Have a Break. Enjoy Every Snap." Caption: Brand Signature Presentation style: • Professional Hollywood commercial storyboard • Advertising agency pitch board • 10 cinematic storyboard frames • White presentation sheet • Thin black borders • Scene numbers • Timestamps • Production captions • Combination of hand-drawn storyboard notes and ultra-realistic colored frames • Premium chocolate commercial • Luxury food advertising • Macro cinematography • ARRI Alexa 35 • Cooke Anamorphic 50mm • HDR • Soft volumetric lighting • Ultra photorealistic • Masterpiece • 8K

items.title: Midjourney v8.2 + Seedance 2.0 Mini --sref 74117661 --stylize 1000 --profile ycjrvus --preview P
items.description: Midjourney v8.2 + Seedance 2.0 Mini --sref 74117661 --stylize 1000 --profile ycjrvus --preview Prompt: The swordswoman @[reference image] launches forward from complete stillness, crossing the space in a single compresse
items.prompt: Midjourney v8.2 + Seedance 2.0 Mini --sref 74117661 --stylize 1000 --profile ycjrvus --preview Prompt: The swordswoman @[reference image] launches forward from complete stillness, crossing the space in a single compressed burst as blue and violet trails tear behind her body. Her blade cuts through the frame, triggering a circular shock explosion filled with orange pigment fragments, followed by several delayed color trails that reveal the full path of the strike. 16 cuts. No background music or score. High-end cinematic 3D realism fused with graphic painterly anime action. Hyper-fast sword choreography, compressed acceleration, chromatic motion trails, explosive shockwave bursts and fractured pigment splashes define every movement. Each strike releases expanding pressure rings, directional color ruptures and sharp ribbon-like trails that remain suspended for a split second before breaking into glowing fragments. Aggressive tracking shots, extreme perspective, rapid orbiting angles and sudden impact close-ups amplify speed while deep negative space, low-key lighting and controlled blue, violet and orange accents preserve strong silhouette readability. Realistic body weight, metallic materials and feature-film rendering ground the action beneath the abstract color physics.

items.title: Built with GPT Image 2 + Omni Flash + Seedance 2.0. I wanted to see if AI could generate a clien
items.description: Built with GPT Image 2 + Omni Flash + Seedance 2.0. I wanted to see if AI could generate a client ready storyboard, not just a few nice looking frames. So I gave it one prompt. The result: ✅ 12-shot storyboard ✅ Realisti
items.prompt: Built with GPT Image 2 + Omni Flash + Seedance 2.0. I wanted to see if AI could generate a client ready storyboard, not just a few nice looking frames. So I gave it one prompt. The result: ✅ 12-shot storyboard ✅ Realistic POV product photography ✅ Camera actions for every scene ✅ ASMR audio cues ✅ Visual doodles ✅ Product features & warranty section ✅ Ready to hand off to a client or video editor No Figma. No Photoshop. No manual layout work. Just one detailed prompt. This is the exact workflow I'd use for product commercials, UGC ads, Amazon listings, Kickstarter campaigns, and client pitches. The full prompt is below if you want to recreate it. Prompt: Create a clean, premium storyboard infographic for a product ASMR unboxing video. Background: White background with a modern minimalist layout. Title: STORYBOARD Product: HAN RIVER WET & DRY 2 IN 1 STEAM IRON Subtitle: ASMR UNBOXING (POV HAND) Create a 12-panel storyboard arranged in a 4×3 grid divided into: PART 1 (0–10s) PART 2 (10–20s) PART 3 (20–30s) Each panel should include: • Number & timestamp • Realistic cinematic image • Handwritten doodle text • Visual • Action • Doodle • Audio Use premium commercial photography with warm studio lighting, shallow depth of field and POV hands only. Finish the infographic with: • What's in the Box • Key Features • Warranty • Ideal For • Metadata section Typography should resemble a premium creative agency storyboard with clean spacing, dark blue accents and thin gray grid lines. (Then include the detailed shot list for all 12 scenes.)

items.title: Seedance 2.0 on @nadoupro Prompt: { "videoprompt": "A first-person POV shot of a hand holding ..
items.description: Seedance 2.0 on @nadou_pro Prompt: { "video_prompt": "A first-person POV shot of a hand holding a black marker, pointing it at real-world objects in an urban city setting. The video uses a creative 'doodle overlay' effec
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 on @nadou_pro Prompt: { "video_prompt": "A first-person POV shot of a hand holding a black marker, pointing it at real-world objects in an urban city setting. The video uses a creative 'doodle overlay' effect. As the hand waves the marker with a swift motion, real-world objects instantly transform into cute, 2D cartoon animations with clean blue and white outlines. 1. A real subway train passing on an elevated track transforms into a playful, minimalist blue and white cartoon train. 2. A real pigeon on a paved sidewalk transforms into a stylized white cartoon bird flying away, leaving small hand-drawn dust clouds. 3. A real green bus driving on a city street transforms into a giant, adorable 2D animated orange tabby cat walking casually. 4. A real woman sitting at a bus stop transforms into a classic 2D anime-style girl waving happily. The final shot shows the hand tossing an object into the sky, transforming the entire city sky into a bright, vibrant anime sunset with fluffy white clouds and a smiling cartoon sun. Smooth transitions, playful and whimsical energy, high-contrast mix of live-action and 2D cel animation." } Frame-by-Frame Generation Prompts If you want to generate the specific static assets or concepts individually, you can use these tailored image prompts: * The Cartoon Train: > A crisp first-person POV photograph of a hand holding a black marker aimed at an elevated city train track. Overlaid on the real scene is a bright 2D vector doodle of a minimalist blue and white cartoon subway train passing by. Playful, mixed media, urban street style. > * The Pigeon Transformation: > First-person perspective looking down at a paved city sidewalk. A hand holds a black marker, pointing at a stylized, hand-drawn 2D white cartoon dove that is mid-flight, bursting out from where a real bird just stood. Whimsical line art overlay. > * The Giant Cat Bus: > A wide POV shot of a city street next to a sidewalk. items.title: Here's how to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation. I did it all in
items.description: Here's how to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation. I did it all in a single chat using the Comfy MCP. The real control here comes from calling my existing workflows (shared below) instead of
items.prompt: Here's how to get 100% consistent product ads from one seedance 2.0 generation. I did it all in a single chat using the Comfy MCP. The real control here comes from calling my existing workflows (shared below) instead of the agent improvising a pipeline. I directed the agent to call my ComfyUI workflow for cinematic product ads. I specified the close-up shot of the sprite animating, the bezel turn flipping the screen, the display changing to the time 10:04. Now for the consistent variations. The driving video does the heavy lifting but you need to get it right → depthanything v3 pass blended with canny edge lines to show the fine detail... it's why the tiny debossed logo is there → the initial sprite outline lived in those edge lines too, and every gen kept inheriting it. claude suggested a sam3 mask over the screen to hide it (s/o the agent) → with the screen masked, the new star sprite is just prompting: one gpt-image-2 still to generate a reference, one extra line in the seedance 2.0 prompt, and it animates oh and the whole process is a claude skill now.

items.prompt: "Don't post this" A creator with early Seedance 2.0 access sent me his render privately I'm posting it Two women fighting in a flooded bathroom. Same face, same dress, same reflection in the mirror through every cut. Full contact from frame one He didn't post this anywhere. Didn't make a tutorial. Just dropped the file in a DM and said "tell me this looks like AI" I couldn't Three documents. That's what separated his output from everyone else's failed generations → Character sheet: face, dress, hair, three angles, locked → Location sheet: bathroom, fluorescent light, water level, locked → Storyboard: every shot in order, locked before a single frame rendered She gets thrown into a door. Water sprays across tile. The camera holds her face. Not one feature drifts Production studios are quietly testing this on real pre-vis budgets. Nobody is posting about it publicly yet You're still rewriting prompts and wondering why your character changes face at frame twelve The prompt was never the problem. The three documents you never built were

items.prompt: Riding into a kingdom where dragons guard the skies and magic fills the air. Created with seedance 2.0 on @Lart_AI Give it a try here 👇  Prompt: A breathtaking cinematic fantasy scene of a beautiful princess wearing an elegant white and gold royal gown riding a majestic white horse through a magical kingdom. The camera starts with a low-angle close-up of the horse's hooves galloping over a glowing cobblestone path, then smoothly pulls back to reveal the princess as her long hair and flowing cape move gracefully in the wind. Colorful flowers bloom along the roadside, butterflies flutter through the air, and sparkling magical particles float around her. In the distance stands a magnificent golden castle surrounded by waterfalls and lush green hills. Two enormous dragons soar gracefully above the castle, their wings casting dramatic shadows as they circle the towers. The princess looks up with a gentle smile while sunlight streams through the clouds, creating god rays and a dreamy atmosphere. The camera performs a slow cinematic orbit before ending on a wide aerial shot of the magical kingdom. Ultra-realistic, fantasy epic, volumetric lighting, soft fog, HDR, highly detailed, cinematic color grading, smooth camera movement, 4K, 24fps, shallow depth of field, masterpiece. items.title: Seedance 2.0 4K on Higgsfield Prompt: @ Image1 is the master character reference. Match her iden
items.description: Seedance 2.0 4K on Higgsfield Prompt: @ Image_1 is the master character reference. Match her identity, facial structure, body proportions, hairstyle, wardrobe, accessories, revolver, colors, textures, silhouette, and ove
items.prompt: Seedance 2.0 4K on Higgsfield Prompt: @ Image_1 is the master character reference. Match her identity, facial structure, body proportions, hairstyle, wardrobe, accessories, revolver, colors, textures, silhouette, and overall presence exactly in every frame. No identity drift, no wardrobe changes, no hairstyle changes, no prop changes, no AI morphing, no flickering, and no inconsistencies. GLOBAL STYLE • Cinematic modern Western • Low golden-hour sunlight from camera-left • Warm amber highlights with cool teal shadows • Fine cinematic film grain • Soft halation around the sun • Long dramatic shadows • Open prairie, dusty dirt road, distant blue mountains • Rising dust illuminated by backlight • Premium Hollywood production quality CHARACTERS • The Stranger (FOCAL): Match @ Image1 exactly. • Lead Bandit: Dusty tan duster, wide-brim brown hat, red bandana covering lower face. • Three background riders: Bandana-masked silhouettes on horseback, partially obscured by dust. SEQUENCE SHOT 1 (0–3s) — HOOK Full side-tracking shot as a stagecoach races across the prairie at full gallop. Four bandits pursue on horseback while firing pistols into the air. Dust explodes behind the coach, glowing in the golden backlight. The camera tracks with the coach before whip-panning down to pounding hooves. SFX: Hoof thunder, gunshots, wooden carriage rattle, tense orchestral strings. Lead Bandit shouts: "Stop the coach!" SHOT 2 (3–6s) — RISING Low-angle tracking shot alongside the speeding coach. The Lead Bandit pushes his horse alongside, leaps onto the moving roof, struggles for balance, crawls toward the carriage door, and grips the rail naturally with realistic weight and momentum. SFX: Boot scraping wood, heavy breathing, rushing wind. SHOT 3 (6–9s) — GREED Close-up of the Lead Bandit's eyes above the bandana, followed by an extreme close-up of his gloved hand wrapping around the brass door handle. He grins confidently before yanking the handle open while riders continue racing behind. SFX: Metal latch click, quiet chuckle. SHOT 4 (9–12s) — THE REVEAL Over-the-shoulder view from behind the Lead Bandit into the dim stagecoach interior. Music stops instantly, leaving only wind and the carriage creaking. Sitting calmly inside is the Stranger, matching @ Image1 perfectly. Hat low, hands relaxed, completely motionless. She slowly lifts her eyes toward him. A sharp beam of golden sunlight cuts across her face and cow-print corset while the rest of the cabin remains in teal shadow. The Lead Bandit's confident grin disappears instantly as his eyes widen and his breathing catches. SFX: Wind, leather creak, carriage springs. SHOT 5 (12–15s) — THE BUTTON Extreme close-up of the Stranger's hand already resting on the ivory-grip engraved revolver. Focus locks on the revolver before snapping into an extreme close-up of her cold eyes beneath the hat brim. She slowly tightens her grip with all five fingers, tilts her head slightly as if silently saying, "Well, hello." Hard cut to black. SFX: Leather creak, one deep cinematic chord, single spur chime. Centered title appears: "THE LAST STAGECOACH" TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS • 15-second duration • 16:9 landscape • Ultra-realistic live action • Hollywood Western film quality • Stable cinematic camera movement • Realistic horse animation and physics • Natural body mechanics • Accurate fabric simulation • Consistent lighting throughout • Perfect facial consistency using @ Image1 • Straight geometry and natural proportions • No AI artifacts • No flickering • No morphing • No duplicated objects • No clipping • No physics errors • No subtitles • No watermark • No logos

SFX: Boot scraping wood, heavy breathing, rushing wind. SHOT 3 (6–9s) — GREED Close-up of the Lead Bandit's eyes above the bandana, followed by an extreme close-up of his gloved hand wrapping around the brass door handle. He grins confidently before yanking the handle open while riders continue racing behind. SFX: Metal latch click, quiet chuckle. SHOT 4 (9–12s) — THE REVEAL Over-the-shoulder view from behind the Lead Bandit into the dim stagecoach interior. Music stops instantly, leaving only wind and the carriage creaking. Sitting calmly inside is the Stranger, matching @ Image1 perfectly. Hat low, hands relaxed, completely motionless. She slowly lifts her eyes toward him. A sharp beam of golden sunlight cuts across her face and cow-print corset while the rest of the cabin remains in teal shadow. The Lead Bandit's confident grin disappears instantly as his eyes widen and his breathing catches. SFX: Wind, leather creak, carriage springs. SHOT 5 (12–15s) — THE BUTTON Extreme close-up of the Stranger's hand already resting on the ivory-grip engraved revolver. Focus locks on the revolver before snapping into an extreme close-up of her cold eyes beneath the hat brim. She slowly tightens her grip with all five fingers, tilts her head slightly as if silently saying, "Well, hello." Hard cut to black. SFX: Leather creak, one deep cinematic chord, single spur chime. Centered title appears: "THE LAST STAGECOACH" TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS • 15-second duration • 16:9 landscape • Ultra-realistic live action • Hollywood Western film quality • Stable cinematic camera movement • Realistic horse animation and physics • Natural body mechanics • Accurate fabric simulation • Consistent lighting throughout • Perfect facial consistency using @ Image1 • Straight geometry and natural proportions • No AI artifacts • No flickering • No morphing • No duplicated objects • No clipping • No physics errors • No subtitles • No watermark • No logos

items.description: A 20-year-old finished a $40,000 animation before her flight started boarding. She was sitting at the airport with one carry-on, an iPhone plugged into the wall, and a lollipop in her mouth. No camera. No editing compute
items.prompt: A 20-year-old finished a $40,000 animation before her flight started boarding. She was sitting at the airport with one carry-on, an iPhone plugged into the wall, and a lollipop in her mouth. No camera. No editing computer. She has never opened Blender. She found a viral 3D animation on TikTok, took a screenshot and uploaded it to ChatGPT with one sentence: “Write the prompt for a video like this.” ChatGPT broke down the lighting, camera movement, textures and character design. She copied the result into an AI video generator. A few minutes later, a pineapple in sunglasses, a crowned strawberry and a blueberry holding a glass were fully animated, voiced and lip-synced. She uploaded the finished short to YouTube before boarding began. A traditional animation studio would need a team, two months and roughly $40,000 to produce the same kind of video. She did it beside an airport charging outlet with a phone in one hand. The production advantage is no longer owning a studio. It is knowing what to show the machine.

items.prompt: Made with Seedance 2.0 Prompt: A cinematic wide shot high above the clouds on a bright sunny day. A giant, fluffy white cloud floats through a deep blue sky with soft wispy clouds. A diverse group of stylish men and women stand in a perfect single-file line on top of the cloud, wearing elegant minimalist fashion in white, brown, cream, and red tones. The camera slowly tracks sideways, creating a calm, dreamlike atmosphere. Suddenly, the first person confidently steps off the edge, diving gracefully before seamlessly flying like a superhero. The others follow one by one in perfect rhythm, soaring through the sky in an organized formation, their clothes flowing naturally in the wind and leaving subtle white vapor trails. Soft natural daylight, surreal luxury fashion editorial aesthetic, photorealistic humans, ultra-smooth cinematic camera movement, volumetric lighting, realistic cloud physics, highly detailed clothing textures, natural motion blur, 8K HDR, masterpiece quality.

items.prompt: llevo un rato dándole vueltas a esto y no me lo creo Seedance 2.0 on Higgsfield AI Prompts y assets open-source completos abajo: o sea el prompt que usaron ELLOS para ESTE video, te lo ponen ahí para que lo copies, así de fácil antes tocabas adivinar cómo lo habían hecho ahora te lo dan todo hecho @Image1 is the lead performer — preserve her exact identity in every shot: cornrow braids, septum ring, statement earrings, sculptural glossy white wrapped top, dark indigo denim. @Audio1 is the music track — entire edit cut to it; no invented music, no voiceover, no lip-sync. Music-video route: dance/performance, rooftop golden-hour editorial. Track: industrial hip-hop, 95 BPM half-time, distorted 808s, luxury-noir. Director thesis: a skyline coronation — the lead and her four dancers own an empty rooftop at last light, silhouettes against the city. Visual world: flat concrete rooftop of a brutalist tower at golden hour, low sun raking long shadows across the roof, city skyline hazy in the background, wind moving fabric. Background dancers: 4 dancers in tonal rust and camel utilitarian looks, sculptural streetwear. Palette: golden amber, rust, concrete, bone white top cutting through, deep indigo. Natural light only, no neon, no particles. Cut map synced to @Audio1 : 0.0–2.0s THE BREATH — static ultra-wide from across the rooftop: five figures spread far apart in a pentagon, all facing the sun, long shadows pointing at camera. Wind and stillness. 2.0s THE DROP — 808 kick: all five pivot to camera in unison on the hit; hard cut to low-angle medium of the lead, sun flaring behind her braids. 2.0–4.0s she struts along the roof edge parapet line (safe, well inside the roof), the four dancers converging on her diagonally from the corners in lockstep, shadows sweeping like clock hands; camera dollying backward. 4.0s snare — whip-pan: full freeze in a tight cluster, she hits a profile vogue arm frame, dancers frozen in four different angular silhouettes against the skyline. 4.0–8.0s golden-hour choreography in medium-wide: her sharp head isolations and New Way vogue arm lines land on the snares; the four dancers frame her in rotating formations — box, diagonal, split pairs — changing formation exactly on each snare hit; torso rolls ripple through the group on the 808 slides. Camera orbits with the sun cross-lighting faces. 8.0s hi-hat triple roll — 3 rapid macro inserts: her earring flaring in backlight, four hands hitting the same vogue frame stacked in depth, boots pivoting on rooftop gravel. 8.5–12.0s escalation: accelerating orbital around the cluster as the dancers whip around her in a fast carousel, her solo arm work speeding up at the axis, long shadows strobing across the lens; speed-ramp slow-motion stretch — hair and fabric floating in wind — then snap back on the kick. 12.0s bass slide — the whole group drops level together into deep sliding lunges across the concrete, camera dutch and low, the sun skimming the roof surface. 13.5–15.0s FINAL BUTTON — hard cut to the opening ultra-wide: pentagon reformed but now all facing camera, the lead center in a frozen vogue pose one arm high, four dancers kneeling on one knee, five long shadows converging at the lens; micro push-in, hold. Loopable. Performance rules: lead stoic, owns the horizon; dancers precise, faces neutral. Continuity: same five people, same wardrobe, same rooftop and light. Audio intent: every cut and formation change on a hit of @Audio1 ; faint foley only (wind, gravel, fabric). Quality bar: expensive editorial music video, natural cinematic grade, no AI gloss, no glow, no lens-flare abuse.
